mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over rolling 3-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS values combined with declining federal funds rates indicate strong consumer spending outpacing monetary tightening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding corporate capacity utilization and rising earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher wage growth, which consistently predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across rolling 24-month windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, forcing S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 70% of high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions at a 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and real GDP indicates synchronized broad-based economic expansion, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined housing and GDP signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment alongside declining payroll employment confirms a deteriorating labor market, triggering recession fears that suppress S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: The dual labor deterioration signal fails to predict negative returns during late-cycle periods, or SPX rallies in greater than 60% of instances despite worsening labor data.
